blockers.title: Own application history is hidden after a member becomes minor or restricted_unknown
blockers.evidence: The spec's dated ruling says own application history stays visible whatever the member's current lane. The backend supports that in convex/opportunities.ts:375-407, but the portal blocks the view: src/portal/PortalShell.tsx:75-85 marks opportunities as youth-blocked, and src/portal/views/OpportunitiesView.tsx:31-58 skips myApplications and returns only a lock state when restricted is true. A member with a past application who later becomes minor/restricted_unknown cannot see her own result or note.
blockers.required_fix: Keep the adult opportunity board/detail/apply gated, but render a history-only My applications surface for minor/restricted_unknown/currently ineligible members. Query myApplications even when restricted, avoid linking those rows into gated opportunity detail/apply flows, and add regression coverage for a current minor/restricted_unknown member with an existing application result.

blockers.title: Platform-health kill criteria are exposed to plain admins despite the super-admin-only spec
blockers.evidence: specs/activity-log.spec.md C10 says admin/overview.getPlatformHealth must call requireSuperAdmin first. The implementation calls requireAdmin at convex/admin/overview.ts:292-296; requireAdmin admits both ADMIN_EMAILS and SUPER_ADMIN_EMAILS in convex/lib/adminAuth.ts:102-113. Reports is visible to all admins in src/admin/AdminApp.tsx:380-383 and renders ReportsView for all admin roles at src/admin/AdminApp.tsx:449-450, where src/admin/views/ReportsView.tsx:33-36 calls getPlatformHealth. tests/convex/activityLog.test.ts:271-359 only deny non-admin and exercise a super admin, so a plain ADMIN_EMAILS admin is not tested.
blockers.required_fix: Either update the vault/spec to explicitly allow plain admins to see platform health, or implement the stated requireSuperAdmin gate and hide or replace the platform-health panel for plain admins. Add a test proving a plain ADMIN_EMAILS admin cannot query getPlatformHealth if C10 remains super-admin-only.

non_blocking_notes: git diff --check main...HEAD passed, aside from sandbox xcrun cache warnings.
The Codex Security preflight helper could not run in this read-only sandbox because xcrun could not write /tmp cache state and the system Python lacks tomllib; the diff was reviewed manually.
Full build and E2E were not run in the read-only sandbox because the project build/test pipeline writes generated artifacts.
No secrets or member-data leaks were found in the reviewed changed source. No member-facing em dash blocker was found in the reviewed source strings.

spec_compliance.criterion: Panel spec dated ruling: own application history stays visible whatever the member's current lane.
spec_compliance.evidence: Server query preserves own history, but the portal suppresses the whole opportunities view and skips myApplications for restricted lanes.

spec_compliance.criterion: Activity-log spec C10: getPlatformHealth is super-admin-only.
spec_compliance.evidence: Implementation uses requireAdmin, ReportsView calls it for all admins, and tests do not cover a plain-admin denial.

spec_compliance.criterion: Events: restricted_unknown sees no events; minors see youth-only; adult/all events are server-filtered.
spec_compliance.evidence: convex/events.ts laneSeesEvent returns false for restricted_unknown, youth_only for minors, and adult/all for standard/ally active members.

spec_compliance.criterion: Events: live audience lane and timing cannot be changed through admin edits.
spec_compliance.evidence: convex/admin/events.ts freezes live audience_lane and rejects start/end time changes for live rows.

spec_compliance.criterion: Opportunities: applications and winner selection re-check current eligibility server-side.
spec_compliance.evidence: convex/opportunities.ts gates apply by active standing, lane, audience, deadline, and profile completeness; convex/admin/opportunities.ts revalidates winner eligibility before recording a winner.

spec_compliance.criterion: Directory: no minor/restricted access and no bio/contact leakage.
spec_compliance.evidence: convex/directory.ts denies minor/restricted viewers and returns limited profile fields without email, mobile, DOB, gender, or bio.

spec_compliance.criterion: Member admin: contact reveal is explicit and audited; default detail view masks sensitive contact data.
spec_compliance.evidence: convex/admin/members.ts masks contact fields in getMemberAdmin and audits revealMemberContact before returning email/mobile.
convex/admin/certificates.ts uses super-admin authorization paths, and the admin shell hides certificates outside isSuper.

spec_compliance.criterion: Reports expose aggregate data only, not member rows.
spec_compliance.evidence: convex/admin/overview.ts report queries return aggregate counts and grouped metrics. The blocker is role scope for platform health, not row-level leakage.

spec_compliance.criterion: Brand locks: member-facing strings added by the diff avoid em dashes and use plain language.
spec_compliance.evidence: No member-facing em dash blocker was found in the reviewed changed source strings.

spec_compliance.criterion: Proof gate: project build passes with zero errors.
spec_compliance.evidence: Typecheck passed, but full build/test/e2e verification was not run in the read-only sandbox.

summary: Do not ship. The slice still violates a core safeguarding/transparency ruling by hiding a member's own opportunity history after a lane change, and it exposes platform-health kill criteria to plain admins despite the super-admin-only acceptance criterion.
